summary.intent: Fix a security bug in moltis where the exec allowlist was not enforced when approval_mode was set to 'never' (Off), meaning headless agents with a configured allowlist had no command restrictions.
summary.outcome: Implemented and merged PR #662 fixing the allowlist enforcement in ApprovalMode::Off, including a follow-up fix for dangerous-pattern commands returning NeedsApproval in Off mode (which would hang headless agents). All 34 tests pass, clippy clean.

repo: Security levels: Full bypasses mode checks entirely (early return at approval.rs:285), Deny rejects everything, Allowlist enforces the list. This layering means Full+Off is always unrestricted.
The project uses 'beads' as its issue tracker CLI (bd create, bd claim commands) alongside GitHub issues.
Rust nightly toolchain pinned to nightly-2025-11-30 for fmt.
just lint fails pre-existing on a CUDA/CMake build error in llama-cpp-sys-2 — use narrow cargo clippy -p <crate> instead.
PR descriptions follow a structured format with summary, validation checklist, manual QA steps, and new test list.

code.finding: Dangerous-pattern regex check runs before mode dispatch. Originally returned NeedsApproval even in Off mode, which hangs headless agents. Fixed in follow-up commit to return Err instead when mode is Off.

SecurityLevel::Full early-returns Proceed before the ApprovalMode match arm, so Full semantics are always unrestricted regardless of mode. The bug was in ApprovalMode::Off arm which unconditionally returned Proceed without checking self.allowlist.
Existing test_approval_off_mode uses empty allowlist — this is why the bug was undetected. The fix preserves empty-allowlist=unrestricted semantics.
Config template for [tools.exec] needed a comment clarifying that allowlist is now enforced under approval_mode='never' when non-empty.

workflow: Greptile automated PR review caught the dangerous-pattern NeedsApproval hang in Off mode — a follow-up that was initially scoped out but was correctly escalated to land in the same PR.
Ephemeral worktree branches have no upstream by default; push with -u flag to set upstream before creating PR.
Commit message convention: fix(crate): description, with 'Fixes org/repo#N' in body, no Co-Authored-By trailer.
Test naming convention encodes the scenario: test_approval_off_with_allowlist_miss_denies style.

friction: just lint fails pre-existing on CUDA/CMake build error in llama-cpp-sys-2, forcing use of narrow cargo clippy per-crate instead of the project-wide lint recipe.
The dangerous-pattern hang issue was initially scoped out as a follow-up but PR review correctly flagged it as in-scope, requiring a second fix commit and push within the same session.
open_items: Config validation warning when approval_mode='never' + empty allowlist + security_level='allowlist' (issue's Option 3 — belt-and-suspenders UX). Not filed as beads issue yet.
Two follow-up beads issues were identified in the plan but not filed: (1) dangerous-pattern NeedsApproval hang — now fixed in PR; (2) config validation warning — still open.
PR #662 targets main but the session ended without confirming CI passed on the CUDA/CMake lint path.
